summary: I was born in Burma in 1993, and my ethnicity is Karen. Although I left Burma at a young age, it is my birthplace and a part of my identity. My early life began there before my family moved to a refugee camp in Thailand.

I grew up in a Thai refugee camp in Chai Mai, Thailand, where I lived for ten years. Life was hard there, and making a living was not easy, so education was very important to us. I attended a Thai middle school with my best friend Wah Paw, and we shared many experiences, including getting punished for wearing our Karen traditional bags.

My family moved to the United States in 2007, starting our new life in Phoenix, Arizona. Later, we relocated to Saint Paul, Minnesota, where I continued my education and graduated from high school. Now, I am a student at Saint Paul College, and I feel proud of how far I have come since arriving in the U.S.

When my family first arrived in the United States on September 13, 2007, we settled in Phoenix, Arizona. It was there that I started attending Cortez High School and began adapting to life in America. Phoenix was the first stop on our journey to building a new life.

After my sophomore year, my family moved to Saint Paul, Minnesota, where I finished high school at Como Park Senior High School. Saint Paul became my new home, and I continued to grow and learn there. Now, I am attending Saint Paul College, which makes me feel connected to this city.
cues: graduated Como Park Senior High School

summary: When we first arrived in Phoenix, Arizona, I started attending Cortez High School. It was my introduction to American education and culture. I spent my freshman and sophomore years there before moving to Saint Paul.

After moving to Saint Paul, Minnesota, I enrolled at Como Park Senior High School, where I completed my high school education. Graduating from there was a proud moment for me and my family. It marked an important milestone in my journey from a refugee camp to the United States.

Currently, I am a student at Saint Paul College, continuing my education and working towards my goals. Attending this college in Saint Paul feels like a continuation of my journey and growth since arriving in the United States. My parents and I are very proud of this achievement.
